titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/184

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=49 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-06 15:19:34.077121+00 | translation moa=50 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-06 15:19:34.062219+00

p.cl:
Alexander . dei gratia rex Scottorum . omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue .
clericis ⁊ laicis  salutem .

Sciant presentes ⁊ futuri . me dedisse . ⁊ concessisse .
⁊ hac carta mea confirmasse . deo ⁊ ecclesie sancte Marie de Melros’ . ⁊ monachis ibidem
deo seruientibus totam terram de Moybothelbeg’ . quam Dunecanus . filius Gilleberti .
eis dedit . per rectas diuisas contentas in carta ipsius Dunecani . ⁊ totam terram de
Bethoc’ . quam idem Dunecanus . eis dedit . per rectas diuisas suas similiter contentas
in carta ipsius Dunecani . Tenend’ in liberam ⁊ puram ⁊ perpetuam elemosinam cum
omnibus aisiamentis . ⁊ aliis ad predictas terras iuste pertinentibus . ita libere
⁊ quiete . plenarie ⁊ honorifice . sicut carta predicti Dunecani . inde facta predictis
monachis  testatur . saluo seruitio meo .

Testibus . Willelmo . episcopo Sancti Andree . Willelmo . de Boscho . cancellario . comite . Patricio . comite . Malcolmo . de fif’ . Willelmo . Cumin’ . comite .
de Buchan’ . Alano . filio . Rollandi . constabulario . Philippo . de Mubray . Waltero . Olifard’ . Adam .
filio Gilleberti . Ingelramo . de Baillol’ . Alexandro . uicecomite . de Striuelin . Waltero . de
Lindes’ . Johanne . de Maccuswel’ .
Alexander, by the grace of God king of Scots, to all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, greeting:

Let present and future know that I have given, granted, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God there, the whole land of Maybole, which Duncan son of Gilbert gave to them, by the correct boundaries contained in the charter of the same Duncan; to be held in pure and pertpetual alms with all easements, and other things justly pertaining to the aforesaid lands, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably, as the charter of the aforesaid Dunca made thereupon for the aforesaid monks bears witness, saving our service.

As witnesses: William, bishop of St Andrews; William del Bois, chancellor; Earl Patrick; Earl Malcolm of Fife; William Comyn, earl of Buchan; Aland son of Roland, constable; Philip de Mowbray; Walter Oliphant; Adam son of Gilbert; Ingram de Balliol; Alexander, sheriff of Stirling; Walter of Lindsay; John of Maxwell.
At Edinburgh, on the second day of October.
